name: Chuck Hagel
summary: Former Secretary of Defense and two-term U.S. Senator from Nebraska who endorsed Kamala Harris in 2024. Hagel, a decorated Vietnam War veteran and lifelong Republican, cited Trump's unfitness for office and threats to national security as reasons for his unprecedented decision to endorse a Democratic candidate.

keyStatements.quote: Donald Trump is a threat to our national security and our democracy.
I have never voted for a Democrat for president, but I will this time because the stakes are too high.

background: Chuck Hagel served as the 24th Secretary of Defense from 2013 to 2015 under President Barack Obama. Before that, he represented Nebraska in the U.S. Senate from 1997 to 2009, where he served on the Foreign Relations and Intelligence committees. A decorated Vietnam War veteran who received two Purple Hearts, Hagel was known as an independent-minded Republican who broke with his party on the Iraq War. He was the first Republican Secretary of Defense to serve under a Democratic president in modern times.
oppositionReasons: Believes Trump poses a direct threat to national security
Concerned about Trump's fitness to serve as Commander-in-Chief
Worried about Trump's authoritarian tendencies and disrespect for democratic norms
Troubled by Trump's relationships with adversarial foreign leaders
Concerned about Trump's treatment of military and veterans
notableActions: Endorsed Kamala Harris for president in 2024 - his first Democratic presidential endorsement
Publicly stated Trump is a threat to national security and democracy
Broke with Republican Party to oppose Trump
Warned about dangers of Trump's leadership to military and national security
credibility: As a decorated combat veteran, former Senator, and Secretary of Defense who served under both Republican and Democratic presidents, Hagel's national security credentials are impeccable. His decision to endorse a Democratic presidential candidate for the first time in his life underscores the severity of his concerns about Trump's fitness to serve as Commander-in-Chief.
